title: [WSL2] [Rootful] Connection timeout trying access an exposed port via host.containers.internal from the container itself
body:
### Issue Description

Creating a container that expose a port and trying to access the exposed port via host.containers.internal (from the container itself) generate a connection timeout.
Accessing others ports exposes by others containers works.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman run --name web1 -d -p 8080:80 nginx
2. podman run --name web2 -d -p 8081:80 nginx
3. podman exec -it web1 bash
4. curl http://host.containers.internal:8081 (Works) (Access to web2 via host.containers.internal)
5. curl http://host.containers.internal:8080 (Don't works) (Connection timeout accessing itself)


### Describe the results you received

Connection timeout accessing itself via http://host.containers.internal:8080

### Describe the results you expected

Working as accessing web2 via http://host.containers.internal:8081

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
